scope.summary: Commits the C6 fixture: the state-store flock is held exactly as a running server holds it, then setup --write and clients revoke run as child processes against the same store. A green half pins that both verbs fail rather than silently succeeding; a committed-red half asserts the failure names the lock instead of emitting the generic write-failure catch-all.
out_of_scope: Mapping FileStoreError::Locked to an actionable code — A2a owns the fix; this bead owns only the fixture.
Naming the specific holder (pid, listen address) in the message. The fixture asserts the class is identifiable and the remedy implied; how much holder detail is safe to surface is A2a's call.
Every other state-mutating CLI verb. Two representative verbs are covered, not an exhaustive sweep.

entry_points.name: oraclemcp setup --write against a server-owned state store
entry_points.trace: c6_state_mutating_cli_verbs_fail_while_the_store_is_owned spawns the real binary with XDG_STATE_HOME pointed at a store this process holds the service-owner flock on, and asserts a non-zero exit

entry_points.name: oraclemcp clients revoke against a server-owned state store
entry_points.trace: same test, second collision case; both verbs are driven as separate child processes so the contention is real rather than simulated in-process

limitations: Scoped test only: cargo test -p oraclemcp --test c6_cli_vs_running_server (1 passed, 1 expected-failure) plus the deliberate red run. The full workspace gate was not run for this commit.
The diagnostic assertion is committed red by design and does not execute in the default run; it is not cited as proof of anything here.

The lock is held by the test process rather than by a spawned `serve`. flock is per open file description so the contention a child sees is identical, but this fixture does not prove a real server's full startup path takes that lock — file_store's own tests cover that.
Two CLI verbs are covered. Other state-mutating verbs likely share the same catch-all and are not asserted here.
follow_ups: A2a must delete the #[ignore] on c6_a_store_collision_names_the_lock_holder_and_the_remedy, leaving the assertions unchanged, and must keep c6_state_mutating_cli_verbs_fail_while_the_store_is_owned green — a diagnostic fix must not turn the collision into a success path.
Consider whether other state-mutating CLI verbs reach the same ConfigOpsError catch-all; if so they inherit the same indistinguishable failure.
